notes: Default whitespace parsing now matches the MMPDB fixture ID behavior.
Gzip SMILES input now matches the MMPDB fixture ID behavior.
Default whitespace parsing now matches MMPDB's tab-file ID behavior.
Default whitespace parsing now matches MMPDB's two-tab-file ID behavior.
Comma parsing is explicit through delimiter="comma"; MMPDB default comma failure text remains CLI follow-up work.
Explicit has_header=True supports MMPDB's whitespace header behavior.
Explicit has_header=True supports MMPDB's gzip whitespace header behavior.
Explicit has_header=True with delimiter="tab" supports MMPDB's tab header behavior.
Explicit has_header=True with delimiter="comma" supports MMPDB's comma header behavior.
Explicit delimiter="to-eol" preserves the rest-of-line ID behavior for MMPDB-style whitespace input.
OEMMPA supports configurable maximum-heavy-atom filtering in the fragmenter.
OEMMPA supports configurable maximum-rotatable-bond filtering in the fragmenter.

OEMMPA validates cut SMARTS shape for connected two-atom cut definitions.
OEMMPA supports MMPDB's one-through-two cut enumeration counts after canonical multi-cut attachment deduplication.

Phase 16 keeps MMPDB fragment-cache reuse deferred; reopen only if indexing reuse, fragment explainability, audit trails, debugging, or CLI reporting require queryable raw fragment rows that the pair/rule/environment tables cannot provide.
OEMMPA converts MMPDB-style cut R-groups into SMARTS and fragments the same variables under mapped attachment labels.
OEMMPA converts multiple MMPDB-style cut R-groups into SMARTS and fragments the same variable sets under mapped attachment labels.
OEMMPA accepts MMPDB-style R-group files through Analyzer fragmentation configuration and matches the equivalent cut SMARTS workflow.
Phase 17 build rejects a missing --cut-rgroup-file with normalized OEMMPA error text; exact MMPDB fragment command wording is intentionally not copied.
OEMMPA covers the same R-group chemistry through structured fragment records; standalone smifrag table columns remain CLI compatibility work.
OEMMPA covers multi-R-group chemistry through structured fragment records; standalone smifrag table columns remain CLI compatibility work.
OEMMPA rejects invalid R-group inputs through Python helper errors rather than smifrag command prefix and argument-number text.
OEMMPA accepts MMPDB-style R-group files through Analyzer configuration; smifrag table formatting remains CLI compatibility work.
Phase 17 covers missing R-group files at the build/stateless input boundary; OEMMPA does not expose MMPDB's standalone smifrag table command.
OEMMPA emits the same phenol one-cut constant and variable orientations under mapped attachment labels.
OEMMPA now collapses equivalent multi-cut attachment label permutations for the MMPDB alkyl-chain cut SMARTS.
OEMMPA supports explicit cut SMARTS behavior; mapped labels are the accepted representation difference from MMPDB's bare wildcard output.
Current C++ tests cover MMPDB's multi-cut count behavior for explicit alkane cut SMARTS.

OEMMPA's MMPDB-compatible storage now stores 46 rules and 336 pair rows versus MMPDB's 47 and 342; the remaining six-row MW rule-environment delta is classified as canonical aromatic encoding collapse.
Deferred to Phase 14 CLI workflow design unless a concrete dataset-building workflow requires MMPDB-compatible index option parsing.
Deferred to future compatibility polish; OEMMPA currently exposes molecule-level fragmentation guards and has not adopted MMPDB variable-size index-option syntax.
OEMMPA can load properties with molecule records, but database refresh parity remains Phase 9.

OEMMPA refreshes rule-environment statistics after property loading and save; current counts are 527 statistics rows versus MMPDB's 533 because six MW rows collapse into equivalent canonical aromatic encodings.
OEMMPA exposes rule-environment statistics through the Python API; source-SMILES environment matching and CLI formatting remain separate follow-up work.
Property-specific rule-environment statistics and MP prediction are available through the Python API.
Deferred to Phase 14 transform-output design; current Python statistics APIs are property-backed and do not emulate MMPDB's no-property CLI table.
OEMMPA supports structured radius filters over stored rule-environment rows; matching environments from an input query SMILES remains follow-up work.
OEMMPA currently supports conservative variable-text substructure filtering rather than MMPDB's full SMARTS query behavior.
Rule-environment statistics support minimum pair count filtering.
OEMMPA deliberately supports safe count/radius where expressions and rejects broader expression syntax until a structured RuleSelectionOptions field is justified.
Rule-environment predictions support explicit score modes for radius and pair-count selection.
OEMMPA emits a persisted predict TSV with stable rule-environment columns rather than copying MMPDB transform command tables.
OEMMPA writes gzip-compressed persisted predict TSV reports; MMPDB transform gzip file naming is not copied directly.
Rule-environment prediction matches the simple Cl to O MW and MP deltas through the Python API.
OEMMPA writes stable rule-environment and supporting-pair TSV detail reports rather than MMPDB's prefix_rules.txt and prefix_pairs.txt shape.
OEMMPA now emits and scores one-cut MMPDB-style hydrogen deletion transforms using explicit [*:1][H] variables.

MMPDB generate documents and enforces a one-cut rule-application boundary; Phase 13.6 evidence tests also record the one-cut CLI control and lower-level weld_fragments cases, so OEMMPA keeps disconnected multi-cut variables unsupported until a separate product-model API is explicitly adopted.
OEMMPA writes stable persisted generation TSV and gzip reports instead of MMPDB's user-selectable --columns/--headers/no-header format.
MMPDB generate can derive missing constant/query pieces from --smiles; OEMMPA Phase 14b keeps persisted generation explicit around source SMILES and selected rule environments.
MMPDB --subqueries expands query fragments and can substantially change output volume; OEMMPA keeps that outside Phase 14b and defers it until a post-14 workflow decision.
OEMMPA summary recounts direct table rows and returns the same internal count categories needed for list recount behavior.
OEMMPA CLI list reports stable persisted summary counts rather than MMPDB's --all title/property/options report.
OEMMPA exposes MMPDB-style R-group SMARTS conversion as Python workflow helpers rather than a standalone CLI command.
OEMMPA reads the first whitespace-delimited R-group field and ignores trailing labels like MMPDB's file workflow.
Standalone stdin command input is deferred to Phase 14 CLI design; helper list/file tests cover the same merged SMARTS conversion semantics.
Python helpers preserve MMPDB bad-input rejection reasons while omitting standalone command numbering and prefix text.
OEMMPA rejects blank and malformed R-group file rows with MMPDB-style line-specific errors.
Phase 17 CLI input handling reports nonexistent --cut-rgroup-file paths through build/stateless workflows; OEMMPA keeps R-group SMARTS conversion as Python helpers rather than a standalone command.
OEMMPA enforces mutually exclusive cut strategy sources at the Analyzer API boundary rather than the standalone rgroup2smarts command parser.
OEMMPA does not expose RDKit's molecule-tuple API, but label-permutation-normalized chemistry matches the RDKit molecule output records.
OEMMPA does not expose RDKit's resultsAsMols=False API directly, but tests reconstruct the same one-cut and two-cut chemistry under OEMMPA constant/variable conventions.
OEMMPA supports the same pattern-restricted chemistry through explicit SmartsFragmentationStrategy configuration rather than a hidden RDKit-style pattern keyword.
OEMMPA's RDKit-like fragment-record projection matches the RDKit test4 record count and three-cut core chemistry.
OEMMPA now supports an RDKit-style cut-bond surface guard.
OEMMPA one-cut and two-cut records union to the max-cuts-two result.
OEMMPA's one-cut and two-cut aminophenol fragment records union to the combined max-cuts-two output, matching the RDKit test7 invariant.
